svg.title: PMET: Motif pair enrichment in promoters (schematic)
svg.desc: Left panel "Target gene set": 4 of 5 target promoters contain the A+B motif pair (80%). Right panel "Genome-wide promoter background": only 1 of 6 background genes contains A+B (17%). Bottom statistical panel: the 2×2 contingency table for A+B (Target 4/1, Background 1/5) → hypergeometric test P(X ≥ k | K, m, N) + BH/Bonferroni multiple-testing correction → motif pairs ranked by adj.p, with A+B significantly enriched. Note: A+B means both motifs co-occur within the same promoter; proximity is not required. FIMO hits with excessive overlap are filtered.
svg.text: PMET: Motif pair enrichment in promoters
Which motif pairs are enriched in target gene promoters versus the genome-wide background?
e.g., heat-shock genes / cell-type up-regulated genes

g.text: promoter region (~2 kb upstream of TSS)

svg.text: 4 of 5 target genes contain the A+B pair (80%)
All genes with usable promoters form the null universe
1 of 6 background genes contains the A+B pair (17%)
PMET pairing: enrichment test per motif pair

g.text: Is the observed enrichment beyond chance?

svg.text: Note: A+B means both motifs co-occur within the same promoter; PMET does not require proximity. FIMO hits with excessive overlap are filtered out.
